item.title: No replacement for the Strait of Hormuz, Kuwait Petroleum head insists
item.description: Chief executive Shaikh Nawaf Al-Sabah said Iran is “holding the world economy hostage” with Hormuz closure

item.title: ConocoPhillips still cautious on new Venezuelan investments, CEO says
item.description: Ryan Lance reiterates company's wait-and-see approach to Venezuela's oil riches

item.title: Shell could take FID on Venezuela gas project in 2026
item.description: Chief executive Wael Sawan hints ‘one or two projects’ could be greenlighted this year

item.title: ‘We need more supply’: Oil CEOs eye shorter project timelines and greater production
item.description: Artificial intelligence, enhanced oil recovery cited as methods for boosting output

item.title: Vietnam seeks to secure imports of Russian LNG
item.description: Asian country has ambitious plans to use more natural gas for power generation

item.title: Equinor begins drilling at second major Brazil pre-salt development
item.description: Six-well campaign aims to have Raia field on stream in 2028

item.title: Why markets could struggle to replace Qatar’s LNG volumes amid US-Iran war
item.description: Cargoes in short supply because of existing contracts, production losses and higher prices, executives at CERAWeek say

item.title: Turkmenistan clears final hurdle for giant gas field expansion plan
item.description: China National Petroleum Corporation subsidiary awarded contract for project aiming to boost Galkynysh field annual gas output by 10 billion cubic metres

item.title: US independent hits gas with Egypt well
item.description: APA's Apache Egyot unit began exploring for gas in Egypt just over a year ago

item.title: SBM Offshore wins FEED contracts for flagship Guyana project
item.description: Longtail will be Guyana's first gas-driven project with a high condensate-to-gas ratio.

item.title: Norwegian gas imports at risk without stable UK North Sea production, OEUK warns
item.description: Shared infrastructure required to import Norwegian gas could be at risk amid declining UK North Sea investment

item.title: Bahrain proposes using ‘all necessary means’ to protect shipping in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Draft resolution still subject to approval by UN Security Council

item.title: Driller poised to acquire five ‘premium’ jack-up rigs in $287 million deal
item.description: Rig acquisition will be completed through BC Ventures, a newly established 50:50 joint venture between subsidiaries of Borr Drilling and its long-term well construction partner in Mexico

item.title: ExxonMobil submits final studies for eighth major Guyana offshore project
item.description: FPSO destined for Longtail field will be able to process up to 290,000 bpd and 1.2 Bcfd

item.title: Oil prices rise again as top Iran officials deny US talks
item.description: Tensions remain high in Middle East despite US President Donald Trump's claim he postponed strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure for five days

item.title: Carlyle and Vitol slam Europe’s ‘schizophrenic’ approach to energy policy
item.description: Investment firm and energy trader say policies ‘all over the place’ have made it difficult to invest with clarity

item.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis is at stalemate, warns ex-US Secretary of Defence
item.description: Retired General James Mattis told executives that only two paths forward for the conflict are continued stalemate or further escalation

item.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis is at stalemate, warns ex-US defence secretary
item.description: Retired General James Mattis told executives that only two paths forward for the conflict are continued stalemate or further escalation

item.title: Iran war disruption ‘not fully priced in’ on oil markets: Chevron CEO
item.description: There is sharp contrast at play between physical and paper markets, according to Mike Wirth

item.title: Europe on schedule to quit Russian gas by late 2027
item.description: Eastern Mediterranean gas and US LNG are expected to free Europe from Russia’s gas

item.title: No incentives to invest in renewable energy, says Equinor executive
item.description: Equinor’s former head of renewables, Irene Rummelhoff, was shocked when she realised how aligned European industry is in its criticism of the EU’s emissions trading system

item.title: TotalEnergies to move nearly $1 billion offshore wind investment to US oil and gas
item.description: Agreement announced by CEO Patrick Pouyanne and Secretary Doug Burgum to redirect nearly $1 billion to gas and LNG

item.title: Aker Solutions wins FEED contract for Baltic carbon capture network terminal
item.description: Norwegian contractor says FEED phase for Klaipeda project scheduled to be completed in third quarter of 2026

item.title: Venture Global in settlement talks for arbitration cases: CEO
item.description: Mike Sabel: would be ‘surprised’ if more settlements fail to emerge after arbitration wins against Shell, Repsol

item.title: Guyana to name new FPSO after region disputed with Venezuela
item.description: Floater will be named Essequibo 1899 in reference to a territorial clash with Venezuela

item.title: Adnoc chief: Strait of Hormuz closure is 'economic terrorism'
item.description: Sultan Ahmed Al Jaber blasted the ongoing closure of Hormuz and said his company and nation stand strong against attacks by Iran

item.title: Chevron sees ‘many decades of opportunity’ in Venezuela
item.description: Fiscal framework has improved to an extent but ‘ambiguity’ in law still exists, says Mike Wirth

item.title: US energy chief says up to 3 million bpd expected from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
item.description: DOE Secretary Chris Wright also touted the new US-Venezuela relationship at the conference

item.title: Trump's domestic and foreign policy contrasts create headache for oil majors
item.description: US president's clarity on domestic energy policy contrasts sharply with foreign policy ambitions

item.title: Technip Energies buys stake in green hydrogen-based project
item.description: DEZiR project expected to start operations in 2030 and produce 80,000 tonnes per annum of e-SAF
